idea_title: Failure Memory for Research Agents for calibration-aware active learning
decision_reason: LLM reviewer council voted KILL: Proposed method performs 3.51% worse than baseline (0.9474 vs 0.9825 accuracy)

metrics.note: Fallback experiment — LLM codegen was unavailable

review:
**Novelty Reviewer** (2/10): Results show 3.51% accuracy degradation and don't test the intended Failure Memory concept, representing a step backward rather than advancement.
**Experimental Reviewer** (2/10): The proposed method significantly underperforms the baseline with a 3.5% drop in accuracy and 3.8% drop in F1-macro. The experiment has fundamental flaws in model comparison and implementation, making the results unreliable.
**Reproducibility Reviewer** (2/10): The proposed method shows significant performance degradation (0.9474 vs 0.9825 accuracy) with only one seed, indicating the approach is not working as intended. The 'fallback experiment' note suggests incomplete implementation.

reviewer_council.revision_suggestion: Implement actual Failure Memory mechanism and test it against baseline using same model architecture (e.g., both LogisticRegression with/without Failure Memory)
experiments_needed: Implement Failure Memory mechanism for research agents
Test on multiple datasets with proper hyperparameter tuning
Compare Failure Memory vs baseline using same model architecture
Re-run experiment with proper implementation of the proposed method
Use multiple seeds (e.g., [42, 123, 456, 789]) for statistical significance
Increase epochs to ensure convergence (e.g., 10-50 epochs)
Compare with additional baselines to establish proper performance benchmarks
Re-run with multiple seeds (5-10) to assess variance
Implement the complete LLM-based failure memory system
Compare against additional baselines on the same dataset
